acceptance_test: Pass if the revised passages anchored at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_01.md:333, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_01.md:405, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_01.md:469 contain only one direct statement that Tomas equals night access, and the later passages each introduce a new consequence or tactic. Fail if two or more of those passages still restate the same ring-key-routes logic in explanatory summary.
problem: The chapter articulates Mina's mixed desire-for-Tomas and desire-for-access logic three separate times in almost the same terms. After the first clear recognition, the later beats explain the same motive again instead of escalating it through new behavior, cost, or strategy, which dulls the chapter turn and makes the obsession feel pre-digested.
rewrite_direction: Tighten and differentiate the repeated access/desire beats at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_01.md:333, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_01.md:405, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_01.md:469. Let the chapter state the calculus plainly once, then use the later passages to add new pressure: a sharper moral recoil, a tactical observation about camp routines, or a concrete next-step impulse tied to staying on the island, rather than another paraphrase of 'Tomas is attractive because he is a route.'

acceptance_test: Pass if, in the revised dock exchange spanning snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_01.md:271 through snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_01.md:391, each of Beto, Tomas, and Imogen has at least one line whose diction clearly marks their register: Beto reads youngest and most impulsive, Tomas reads most work/local-specific, and Imogen reads most managerial. Fail if any two of those speakers could still swap the cited lines without sounding conspicuously wrong.
problem: Beto, Tomas, and Imogen all land on a similarly polished dry-zinger cadence in the dock material, which narrows the voice separation the style bible asks for. The scene preserves their broad roles, but the sentence music is close enough that Beto's youthful clowning, Tomas's work-grounded local dryness, and Imogen's managerial edge blur together more than they should.
rewrite_direction: Retune the exchange around snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_01.md:271, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_01.md:277, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_01.md:327, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_01.md:391 so each speaker's wit is rooted in their style-bible register. Push Beto toward faster, slightly overtalking camp/kitchen/dock slang; push Tomas toward clipped county-work, tide, fuel, or logistics phrasing; and let Imogen's line sound more donor-budget-compliance managerial than like another clean one-liner.

acceptance_test: Pass if only one of the revised passages anchored at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_01.md:69, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_01.md:151, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_01.md:405, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_01.md:471 still uses the explicit negation-correction formula, and the other three convey the shift without it. Fail if three or more of those targeted passages still rely on the same template.
problem: The narration leans repeatedly on an explicit negation-and-correction template ('It was not X. It was Y.' / 'It was not only X. It was Y.'), and by the fourth pass it reads as a prose tic rather than a controlled rhetorical choice. That recurrence flattens moments that should sharpen through image or syntax alone.
rewrite_direction: Revise the targeted beats at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_01.md:69, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_01.md:151, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_01.md:405, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_01.md:471 so no more than one keeps the bare negation-reset construction. In the others, let the turn arrive through fresh sensory detail, pressure, or sentence shape instead of another 'not X / but Y' correction.

acceptance_test: PASS if revised lines 561-581 show Tomas performing a specific illegal county act on the page (false entry written, county fuel dispensed, or illicit departure launched) while Mina witnesses it; FAIL if the chapter still ends with both cans empty and the ledger line blank.
problem: The chapter ends with the gas cans still empty and the falsified ledger line still blank, so the irreversible county violation has not actually happened on the page. That contradicts the continuity sheet's canonical chapter-02 state transition that Tomas starts falsifying fuel use and giving Mina illicit night access here, and it weakens the chapter's consequence by deferring the ugliest operational act to chapter_03.
rewrite_direction: Revise the ending in snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_02.md:561-581 so Tomas commits the first irreversible county offense before Mina leaves: either he writes the false log entry at the station, fills the cans with the county key, or initiates the run itself in this chapter. Keep Mina present for the choice so the chapter closes on witnessed consequence rather than anticipation.

acceptance_test: PASS if, in revised lines 241-285, Tomas has at least two leverage turns grounded in county or fuel-work specifics and Mina has at least two replies grounded in the creek/rumor/body-specific want that is hers; FAIL if their lines in that span still read like interchangeable hard-boiled flirtation once dialogue tags are removed.
problem: In the core bargaining stretch, Tomas drifts toward abstract, polished seducer talk ('which part you think is free,' 'Some airboat attached to a pulse,' 'You're expensive in a whole different way') and Mina answers in the same sharpened register. That blurs idiolect separation and nudges Tomas toward generic dangerous-man dialogue instead of the work-rooted county-boatman voice required by the style bible.
rewrite_direction: Rework the bargaining beats in snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_02.md:241-285 so Tomas's leverage comes through county-work diction, fuel-log risk, route knowledge, overtime/job anxiety, and local shorthand, while Mina's replies stay anchored to creek-obsession, bodily want, and shame. Strip or replace the most generic sexy-threat lines rather than polishing them further.

acceptance_test: PASS if, across revised lines 243, 443, 499-500, and 535-536, no more than one beat still uses the sentence-initial 'Not ...' denial/reframe construction; FAIL if the chapter keeps stacking the same correction formula in three or more of those locations.
problem: The prose leans repeatedly on the same negation-to-redefinition sentence shape ('Not flirtation exactly. Not yet.' / 'Not guilt exactly. Not dismissal.' / 'Not triumph. Something rougher...' / 'Not a fall, not a seduction, not fate.'). Once or twice this is a choice; at this frequency it becomes a chapter-level tic and makes the voice feel patterned rather than freshly observant.
rewrite_direction: Vary the sentence logic in snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_02.md:243, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_02.md:443, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_02.md:499-500, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_02.md:535-536. Preserve the emotional meaning, but deliver at least three of those beats through different syntax, image, or action instead of sentence-initial denial/reframe.

acceptance_test: Pass if, by snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_03.md:465-471, the reader can identify a concrete notebook action tied to the sighting: either Mina has written at least one usable field marker from the creek, or the narration explicitly states why she cannot make herself write. Fail if the notebook is still blank with no on-page reason.
problem: The chapter primes Mina's notebook as essential field gear, then never lets her use it or consciously refuse it after the sighting. Because a detail-driven, professionally damaged focalizer ends the night with the notebook still blank, a key procedural impulse drops out of the scene without explanation, which weakens the causal honesty of her obsession and the aftermath of the sighting.
rewrite_direction: In snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_03.md:185-221 or snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_03.md:419-421, add a brief on-page choice about the notebook: Mina either jots concrete markers from the sighting (the root gate, pale twig, nest position, approximate depth) or deliberately leaves the page blank for a stated emotional or procedural reason. Reinforce that choice again at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_03.md:465-471 so the blank-or-written notebook reads as consequence, not oversight.

acceptance_test: Pass if, across the revised spans above, no more than two transition paragraphs open by labeling the preceding beat with an abstract-noun/pronoun frame such as 'The exchange', 'The line', 'The sentence', 'The answer', or 'That landed'. The remaining transitions must land through observable action, sensation, or direct thought on the page.
problem: A repeated transition formula starts to flatten the chapter's otherwise vivid prose: the narration keeps labeling the prior beat with abstract nouns or pronouns ('The exchange', 'The line', 'The sentence', 'The answer') before explaining its effect. Once this happens across multiple turns, the prose steps outside the scene to annotate pressure instead of letting pressure land through body, motion, or sound.
rewrite_direction: Rework the transition beats at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_03.md:101-103, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_03.md:131-133, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_03.md:249-253, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_03.md:275-276, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_03.md:313-319, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_03.md:379-381, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_03.md:443-445 so more of them begin from immediate sensation, gesture, interruption, or direct free-indirect thought. Vary the bridge shapes; do not keep relying on 'The [abstract noun]...' or 'That [verb]...' to summarize the last line.

acceptance_test: Pass if the revised passages anchored at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_04.md:9, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_04.md:11, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_04.md:77, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_04.md:79, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_04.md:287, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_04.md:289 contain no emphatic verbless narration fragments; fail if any beat equivalent to "Too quick" or "Not cold. Not warm. Exact." remains as fragmentary sentence compression.
problem: The chapter repeatedly uses clipped, verbless narration for emphasis ("Too quick." / "Not annoyed. Measuring." / "Not cold. Not warm. Exact."), but the constitution explicitly forbids elliptical clipping in narration. The compression reads like a stylistic shortcut rather than earned bluntness.
rewrite_direction: Recast the fragment beats at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_04.md:11, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_04.md:79, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_04.md:289 into grammatically complete sentences that keep Mina's close-third pressure and Rhea's appraisal sharp without dropping subjects or auxiliaries. Check the immediately adjacent sentences in those exchanges for any similar fragment carryover and repair them in the same pass.

acceptance_test: Pass if, across snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_04.md:77, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_04.md:165, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_04.md:167, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_04.md:171, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_04.md:287, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_04.md:357, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_04.md:411, there is no more than one direct "too quick/too fast" formulation and no more than one direct "exact" label; fail if the revision still relies on repeated keyword tells instead of varied embodied cues.
problem: Mina's pressure and attraction are rendered too often through the same literal stress-tell vocabulary: she is "too quick/too fast" four times, then both Rhea and Mina name her as "exact." Because the style bible already identifies those as Mina/Rhea stress markers, this reuse feels mechanically profile-driven instead of freshly embodied on the page.
rewrite_direction: Vary the pressure cues in the local beats at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_04.md:77, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_04.md:165, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_04.md:167, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_04.md:171, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_04.md:287, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_04.md:357, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_04.md:411. Keep at most one direct speed-tell and one direct "exact" naming, and let the other moments show Mina's stress or desire through distinct actions, sensory shifts, or task-specific behavior tied to bats, coffee, the thin-walled house, or Tomas's offstage presence.

acceptance_test: Pass if the revised ending anchored at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_04.md:305, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_04.md:319, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_04.md:355, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_04.md:385, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_04.md:397, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_04.md:403 states the risk once and makes Mina's agreement to tomorrow night carry a concrete on-page cost or boundary; fail if the scene still ends with a largely frictionless "Yeah" after a second generalized reminder that secrecy is not privacy.
problem: The breakfast scene names the camp's surveillance and power asymmetry clearly, but it spends multiple beats explaining the risk and then resolves into an immediate reinvitation for tomorrow night. That gives the chapter's warning more thematic clarity than practical cost, so the ending hands Mina forward motion without making her absorb a sharper consequence or boundary.
rewrite_direction: In the breakfast confrontation and exit anchored at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_04.md:305, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_04.md:319, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_04.md:355, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_04.md:385, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_04.md:403, keep Rhea's warning and the tomorrow-night hook, but compress the duplicated explanation and attach the invitation to a concrete changed condition: a stated boundary, a new leverage point, or a specific risk Mina has to accept before she says yes.

acceptance_test: Before the first kiss at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_05.md:389, the revised chapter must show one observable action or decision caused by the tunnel discovery and name the cost of that action in scene. If a reader cannot point to a specific protocol change, sample, note, conceal/report choice, or data implication on the page before sexual contact begins, this finding remains unresolved.
problem: The tunnel reveal establishes a serious scientific and ecological consequence, but once that consequence is named, the chapter mostly converts it into atmosphere. Rhea's discovery that her contamination baselines may be compromised never forces a concrete procedural action before the scene pivots into erotic negotiation, so the finding's material cost stays more spoken than felt.
rewrite_direction: At snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_05.md:185 and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_05.md:205, then again before the first kiss at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_05.md:389, add a concrete consequence beat tied to the tunnel itself: Rhea records a specific contamination risk, bags residue, marks the intake geometry, or states what data or permit claim is now compromised. Let that action keep shaping the negotiation instead of leaving the tunnel consequence deferred until snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_05.md:483.

acceptance_test: Across the targeted beats at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_05.md:121, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_05.md:295, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_05.md:395, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_05.md:415, no more than one passage may rely on an explicit expectation-negation-reversal structure to deliver the beat. The others must communicate desire or shame without first announcing and then rejecting an expected reading.
problem: The prose repeats the same expectation-negation-reversal move often enough to become a tic: the narration names what a moment should have been, rejects it, then supplies the dirtier truth. That pattern works as Mina's sensibility once or twice, but repeated across desire and shame beats it starts to feel precomposed rather than freshly perceived.
rewrite_direction: Rework the repeated reversal beats at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_05.md:121, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_05.md:295, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_05.md:395, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_05.md:415. Keep at most one full 'should have/wasn't/it was' self-correction and rewrite the others into direct sensory, behavioral, or syntactic alternatives that move forward instead of correcting themselves.

acceptance_test: In the revised versions of snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_05.md:183, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_05.md:253, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_05.md:355, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_05.md:381, a reader must be able to infer the character state from behavior on the page without abstract labels such as 'calibration,' 'controlled syntax,' 'overname,' or 'instrument.' If those beats still explain the voice instead of embodying it, this finding fails.
problem: Several sentences explain character voice with diagnostic shorthand that reads like a style sheet leaking into the prose: 'calibration,' 'controlled syntax,' 'overname,' 'instrument.' Instead of letting Mina and Rhea's habits emerge from scene, the narration occasionally labels them in abstract terms, which flattens the immediacy the style bible calls for.
rewrite_direction: Replace the meta-labeling at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_05.md:183, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_05.md:253, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_05.md:355, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_05.md:381 with local behavior and sensory consequence. Show what Rhea does with breath, gaze, notebook, touch, or diction in those beats, and show Mina's stress through the actual overspecified words or bodily hitch rather than explanatory summary tags.

acceptance_test: Pass if the revised deadline sequence anchored at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_06.md:237, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_06.md:329, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_06.md:409 lets a reader point to at least one specific material loss Mina will face within the week; fail if the scene still frames the consequence only as donor optics, utility, damage, or general financial pressure.
problem: The chapter renders reputational humiliation vividly, but when Imogen reduces Mina's future to whether her "utility currently exceeds [her] damage," the prose retreats into institutional arithmetic instead of making Mina's personal economic exposure concrete. Given the continuity sheet's debt load and housing precarity, this reads as sanitization of class shame: the moment that should bite hardest stays abstracted into donor optics, camp finance, and generic usefulness.
rewrite_direction: Revise the dismissal and aftermath beats at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_06.md:237, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_06.md:239, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_06.md:313, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_06.md:329, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_06.md:409 so Mina's close-third reaction includes at least one exact, embarrassing material consequence of losing the job after spoonbill week: the payment-plan installment, the bed, where her clothes go, or an equivalent near-term cost. Keep the pressure bodily and specific rather than restating camp-wide abstractions about money or usefulness.

acceptance_test: Pass if the revised passages at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_06.md:45, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_06.md:309, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_06.md:409, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_06.md:461 read as complete clauses or sentences with explicit grammatical cores; fail if the chapter still uses repeated impact fragments such as stacked nouns or bare two-word verdicts to carry its strongest beats.
problem: The chapter repeatedly leans on bare noun fragments and clipped emphasis beats (for example, "Linen hats. Good binoculars." and "Employer. Housing. Guests. Schedules. Need."). That pattern crosses from compression into prohibited clipped narration, violating the constitution's explicit ban on article-stripping and sentence skeleton drop-outs. Because it recurs throughout the chapter, it reads as a drafting tic rather than a controlled one-off emphasis.
rewrite_direction: Rewrite the clipped passages at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_06.md:45, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_06.md:193, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_06.md:309, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_06.md:409, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_06.md:461 into grammatically complete sentences or clauses that preserve compression without relying on stacked noun fragments. Keep the blunt rhythm, but restore subjects and verbs where the chapter is currently dropping them for impact.

acceptance_test: Pass if, across the revised conflict beats centered on snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_06.md:151, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_06.md:247, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_06.md:301, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_06.md:351, at least three major pressure shifts are rendered through non-gaze beats and no two adjacent turns depend on a look/looked/looking construction; fail if the scene still advances mainly through repeated eye-line cues.
problem: The chapter overuses gaze choreography as its default reaction beat. Power shifts repeatedly arrive through who looked, did not look, or looked back, which starts to flatten scene motion into eye-line management instead of letting bodies, objects, sound, or Mina's interior pressure carry the turn. The repetition is noticeable enough to register as autopilot rather than deliberate patterning.
rewrite_direction: In the high-pressure exchanges anchored at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_06.md:23, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_06.md:151, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_06.md:195, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_06.md:247, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_06.md:301, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_06.md:351, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_06.md:367, replace several look/looked/looking beats with more specific physical or sensory reactions: hand placement, breath changes, object handling, posture shifts, noise, or sharper free-indirect response from Mina. The goal is not to remove every gaze beat, but to stop relying on the same one for each turn.

acceptance_test: Reading the revised study sequence at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_07.md:27, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_07.md:79, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_07.md:165, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_07.md:173 should leave no cross-system mismatch: if the text says diesel, no carburetor, venturi, or choke terminology remains; if the text keeps carburetors, the workbook or exam is explicitly small-engine or gasoline-based.
problem: The Beto/Tomas study beat muddies engine systems. The chapter names a diesel-engine workbook, then pivots to carburetors, venturi, choke, and altitude-compensation talk that belongs to gasoline or small-engine instruction instead of diesel. In a book whose diction and character voices depend on local technical exactness, that slip weakens both Beto's ambition beat and Tomas's competence.
rewrite_direction: Unify the mechanical vocabulary across snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_07.md:27, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_07.md:79, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_07.md:165, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_07.md:173. Either keep the workbook and exam in gasoline or small-engine territory so carburetor/venturi/choke detail is correct, or keep the diesel specificity and replace the study problem with injection, compression, or fuel-system detail Tomas could plausibly teach by sound.

acceptance_test: Across the revised passages at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_07.md:359, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_07.md:389, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_07.md:423, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_07.md:433, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_07.md:489, no more than two paragraphs may hinge on 'the room' as the main grammatical subject, and at least three of those beats must instead begin from Mina's body, a concrete object in the bedspace, or a specific action by Tomas or Rhea.
problem: The chapter keeps returning to 'the room' as the abstract subject that receives pressure, smell, or meaning. That framing move lands once, but by the intimacy and aftermath stretch it has become a repeated sentence chassis, flattening escalation into the same atmospheric gesture instead of letting bodies, objects, and specific motions carry each turn.
rewrite_direction: Vary the framing in the pressure beats anchored at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_07.md:253, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_07.md:359, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_07.md:389, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_07.md:423, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_07.md:433, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_07.md:489. acceptance_test: In the revised passages anchored at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_08.md:281 and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_08.md:403, Tomas's class/work grievance and Mina's loss of privacy must be inferable from dialogue, gesture, and concrete objects without standalone narrator-summary sentences naming the underlying theme. problem: At several peak-pressure moments the narration stops to interpret subtext the scene has already made legible. Tomas's labor-risk turn and the closing privacy turn arrive as thematic captions rather than consequences suffered in real time, which slightly bleaches the chapter's otherwise raw confrontation.
rewrite_direction: Rework the interpretive summary beats at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_08.md:239, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_08.md:281, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_08.md:339, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_08.md:403, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_08.md:405, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_08.md:407 so Tomas's county exposure and Mina's loss of privacy land through action, silence, object handling, or bodily reaction already present in the scene. acceptance_test: After revision of the cited lines, no more than two sentence-openers in those spans may use the `That was ...`, `There it was ...`, or `It was ...` formula. Each revised line must still convey the same beat through concrete image, physical sensation, or action rather than a repeated metacommentary frame.
problem: The chapter overuses the same sentence-opening formula (`That was ...`, `There it was ...`, `It was ...`) at major turns. Repeating that frame this often makes the prose sound self-captioning and reduces the rhythmic surprise the style bible calls for.
rewrite_direction: Recast the opener formulas at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_08.md:7, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_08.md:59, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_08.md:65, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_08.md:119, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_08.md:145, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_08.md:265, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_08.md:281, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_08.md:371, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_08.md:405 into more situation-specific syntax tied to motion, sensation, or image. acceptance_test: PASS if the revised material at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_09.md:329, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_09.md:343, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_09.md:355, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_09.md:381, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_09.md:419 gives Mina, Tomas, and Rhea distinct concrete stakes and states the chapter's meaning explicitly only once after the eyewitness debate begins; FAIL if the passage still repeats the same burden/resource/fact thesis in multiple paraphrases after the pact is made.
problem: Once the eyewitness turn lands, the lab aftermath keeps restating the same thematic conclusion in abstract thesis nouns: the sighting becomes a "resource," then a "burden," then a question of "price," then another meditation on "truth" and "fact." The scene still contains conflict, but too much of its final pressure is spent explaining what the chapter means instead of forcing a fresher, materially different consequence.
rewrite_direction: Rework the aftermath centered on snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_09.md:329, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_09.md:343, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_09.md:349, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_09.md:365, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_09.md:381, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_09.md:419 so the scene keeps one clear articulation of the burden, then advances through a new concrete beat: a specific custody precaution, a sharper county-exposure detail, or a camp-risk calculation that changes what they do next. Cut or recast the duplicate abstract paraphrases and let the no-cloud / no-text pact and the interpersonal fracture carry more of the load.

acceptance_test: PASS if the revised sentences at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_09.md:31, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_09.md:187, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_09.md:203, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_09.md:293, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_09.md:349, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_09.md:423 leave no more than two beats using an "It was not..." or "not X but Y" frame and each cited beat reads with a clearly different cadence; FAIL if three or more of those moments still rely on the same negation/correction setup.
problem: A negation-and-correction sentence pattern recurs across major beats: "It was not..." and closely related "not X but Y" constructions keep showing up to announce importance. Used this often, the syntax stops feeling chosen for the moment and starts reading like a house formula, which flattens the chapter's sentence music at exactly the beats that should feel most distinct.
rewrite_direction: Recast the sentences anchored at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_09.md:31, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_09.md:187, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_09.md:203, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_09.md:293, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_09.md:349, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_09.md:423 so Imogen's authority, the eel's recognition, the wake impact, the file analysis, and Mina's final debt beat arrive in materially different sentence shapes. Keep at most two negation/correction turns in the whole set and replace the rest with positive, image-led, or compression-led syntax.

acceptance_test: In the revised material anchored at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_10.md:395, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_10.md:523, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_10.md:605, the chapter states the ownership/asset conversion only once. The surrounding beats must communicate that shift through observable action or image such as invoices, task assignment, Beto's roof line, or the clearance order. Fail if two or more separate lines in those spans still paraphrase the same proof/asset/ownership/debt thesis.
problem: In a chapter whose spine is bureaucratic comedy, the narration and dialogue keep restating the same thesis about proof, ownership, and institutional appetite after the scene has already dramatized it. The invoices, Beto's roof line, the task list, and the clearance order already show the eel becoming camp property; repeating the argument turns comic pressure into explanation and flattens the chapter's tonal commitment.
rewrite_direction: Tighten the explanatory beats at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_10.md:395, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_10.md:401, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_10.md:523, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_10.md:605, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_10.md:613. Keep one decisive articulation of the eel's conversion into institutional matter, then let the invoices, Beto's interruption, the task list, and the NO OUTSIDE CALLS order carry the rest. Replace the other thesis statements with behavior, interruption, or a fresh image that preserves pressure without re-summarizing the chapter's meaning.

acceptance_test: Before or during the Pelican-case reveal anchored at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_10.md:411 and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_10.md:449, the revised chapter must show clear causality for Tomas obtaining the gear and at least one concrete cost beyond retrospective explanation. Pass if a reader can point to the exact beat where the borrow becomes dangerous on page; fail if the case still appears as an unseeded checklist answer and the risk is only narrated after the reveal.
problem: The second scene identifies cleaner equipment and county cover as the next obstacle, then solves both almost immediately by having Tomas arrive on cue with the exact camera, duplicated media setup, and custody envelopes required. The audit danger is explained afterward, but the reveal still reads as an authorially delivered answer more than an earned escalation.
rewrite_direction: Rework the handoff around snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_10.md:405, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_10.md:411, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_10.md:423, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_10.md:449 so Tomas's county borrow is seeded before the reveal or arrives with an immediate on-page complication. Options: establish earlier that he has gone to the dock for something risky, or make his entrance bring a concrete cost such as a supervisor call, a missing-asset concern, a fuel discrepancy, or a sharper confrontation over the sign-out number before the gear is accepted as solution.

acceptance_test: Across the revised beats anchored at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_10.md:281, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_10.md:473, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_10.md:499, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_10.md:573, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_10.md:585, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_10.md:591, no more than two sentences may use the 'Not X ... Y' corrective formula. The remaining contrast beats must read as distinct syntactic choices grounded in scene-specific image or action. Fail if three or more near-identical negation pivots remain.
problem: The chapter overuses the same negation-and-correction sentence template: contrast is repeatedly delivered as some version of 'Not X. Y.' or 'Not X exactly. Y.' What begins as a sharp rhythmic choice becomes a visible tic, especially in the back half, and starts to feel mechanically reused rather than scene-discovered.
rewrite_direction: Vary the syntax at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_10.md:281, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_10.md:473, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_10.md:499, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_10.md:573, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_10.md:585, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_10.md:591. acceptance_test: PASS if the revised bat-lab scene beginning at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_11.md:243 ends with a state change specific to Mina-Rhea-Tomas, the revised Imogen scene beginning at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_11.md:459 ends with a different camp-level consequence visible on-page, and a reader can name two separate new outcomes before the hook at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_11.md:617. problem: The chapter's second half broadens explanation more than it generates fresh turns. Rhea's scene and Imogen's scene both tell Mina, in different vocabularies, that her obsession now carries economic and relational cost; because those scenes do not separate their functions sharply enough, the aftermath starts to feel reiterative instead of escalating.
rewrite_direction: Differentiate the post-dock scenes anchored at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_11.md:243 and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_11.md:459. acceptance_test: PASS if the revised passages around snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_11.md:297 and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_11.md:571 each deliver at least one concrete material or bodily consequence on-page and no scene relies on more than one brief thesis sentence to explain Mina's guilt. evidence: Rhea's speeches at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_11.md:297, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_11.md:303, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_11.md:321 are followed by Imogen's explicit thesis language at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_11.md:571, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_11.md:573, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_11.md:605, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_11.md:613.
problem: Sanitization drift appears here as moral safety-valve explanation. The chapter already has concrete damage on the page, but instead of trusting humiliation, denied access, money objects, and altered touch to carry the ugliness, it repeatedly translates the mess into explicit lesson language about scale, cost, and accounting. That over-clarification softens the book's central contamination of desire, class need, and use by telling the reader what the chapter means instead of letting the consequences stay nastier.
rewrite_direction: At snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_11.md:297, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_11.md:321, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_11.md:571, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_11.md:605, cut back the abstract summary language and move the pressure into action: a concrete household change, a practical refusal, a humiliating money detail, a schedule consequence, or a refusal of touch that lands without thematic gloss. Keep at most one short line of overt summary per scene and let the rest of the damage register through bodies, paperwork, and changed behavior.

acceptance_test: PASS if the revised speech at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_11.md:497 no longer pairs miracle or scandal rhetoric against maintenance in the style-bible example's structure and still sounds recognizably Imogen through concrete camp-finance language. Even though the wording is not verbatim, the same miracle/scandal/maintenance funding contrast is still doing the work, which makes the dialogue feel prompted rather than freshly spoken. Example lines are supposed to calibrate voice, not be paraphrased back into the manuscript.
rewrite_direction: Replace the sentence cluster at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_11.md:497 with new donor-budget language specific to this scene's invoices, repairs, and board panic. Do not reuse the miracle/scandal/maintenance contrast or a close paraphrase of it; let Imogen sound like herself through camp-finance precision and donor pragmatism instead.

problem: Two sentence defaults are flattening the chapter's texture: smell-first scene entry and near-miss emotional hedging. Either move can work once or twice, but at this frequency they start to feel like autopilot scaffolding, especially in a chapter that should differentiate county humiliation, bat-lab intimacy, and office-accounting dread with sharper rhythmic variety.

problem: A chapter can use one hammering parallel structure to land obsession or shame. Here the same sentence engine recurs across several revelation beats, so Mina's self-indictment begins to feel mechanically reiterated after the core discovery is already clear.

problem: This is sanitization-by-summary at the chapter's sharpest pressure point. Mina has just made the self-damaging confession that should expose her to the ugliest public scrutiny, but the inquiry turns into montage before the room's hardest reaction is fully lived on the page. The result slightly softens the ordeal and reduces the social abrasion that should earn the firing.

evidence: A repeated construction turns bureaucratic language into a physical object or force at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_16.md:17, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_16.md:151, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_16.md:229, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_16.md:265, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_16.md:343.
problem: The first instance is strong, but the chapter keeps returning to the same word-as-substance move: words lie on pages, phrases slide under skin, words spread across platforms, vocabulary changes on land. Repeated this often, the motif stops feeling freshly observed and starts reading like a house sentence shape, which thins Mina's embodied close-third voice into abstract commentary.
rewrite_direction: Keep only the strongest one or two instances, then revise the later recurrences at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_16.md:151, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_16.md:229, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_16.md:265, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_16.md:343 so institutional language changes the scene through observable consequence: a stopped pen, a shifted chair, a tightened throat, a donor interrupting, or Mina feeling the change in her body.

acceptance_test: Pass if the revised sentences at the cited lines still carry pressure without relying on the same repeated 'as if' explanation turn in most of those places. Fail if the revised chapter continues to use that identical comparative hinge across the majority of the flagged beats.
evidence: The chapter repeatedly leans on the same explanatory 'as if' hinge at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_16.md:21, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_16.md:33, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_16.md:89, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_16.md:101, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_16.md:113, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_16.md:161, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_16.md:289, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_16.md:311.
problem: At this frequency, the prose starts explaining its images instead of trusting them. The repeated 'as if' turn becomes a visible fallback construction: object or gesture, then comparative gloss telling the reader what emotional weight to assign. That repetition flattens rhythm and creates autopilot prose in a chapter that otherwise has strong sensory authority.

acceptance_test: Pass if the introductions at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_16.md:139 are rendered in complete narration that still differentiates June, Mara, and Elaine by action and register. Fail if the paragraph still presents the introductions as a sequence of clipped labels without full sentence movement.

problem: That paragraph violates the constitution's ban on elliptical clipping in narration and briefly reads like screenplay shorthand. In a chapter built on supple close-third control, the fragmentary introduction breaks cadence just to move names into place.
rewrite_direction: Rewrite snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_16.md:139 as grammatically complete narration with verbs and focalized motion. Keep the pace brisk, but let each arrival enter through action and Mina's perception rather than a stack of verbless labels.

acceptance_test: PASS if the revised passage at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_17.md:197, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_17.md:279, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_17.md:337 leaves deletion ethically unresolved on the page: at least one speaker names a specific protection or knowledge they are losing by destroying the coordinate, no line explicitly labels the act as courage or responsibility, and the text immediately after the delete action still carries one concrete downside rather than thematic closure. FAIL if the scene still reads as a fully argued proof that deletion is simply the right answer.
evidence: snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_17.md:207 frames the GPS dispute in grant-and-permit terms; snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_17.md:217 and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_17.md:221 argue the anti-disclosure case; snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_17.md:273 explicitly defines deletion as courage rather than cowardice; snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_17.md:301 and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_17.md:305 execute the deletions after the scene has already morally ratified them; snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_17.md:337 and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_17.md:349 further sand down the report into a tidy protection logic.
problem: The coordinate-deletion climax over-justifies itself on the page. Instead of leaving the choice ethically compromised, the scene repeatedly explains why deletion is the responsible, courageous answer and casts every institutional alternative as predatory. That moral safety valve reduces the ugliness of what they are actually doing: withholding precise data, planning to sandbag a report, and choosing opacity at the point of highest consequence. In this book's risk profile, that is sanitization drift.
rewrite_direction: Revise snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_17.md:197, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_17.md:217, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_17.md:273, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_17.md:279, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_17.md:337 so the GPS argument stays morally double-edged. Keep the deletion, but cut or roughen the thesis-like justifications, add a concrete protection or scientific use they are sacrificing by deleting the mark, and let at least one character articulate the risk that this choice may also fail the creek later. Keep the exchange anchored to the live scene rather than letting it resolve into a clean statement of principle.

The overview of Tomas and Rhea's damaged arrangement and Rhea's visits to Mina's room explains the new shape of the triangle, but it does so in compressed retrospect rather than through one concrete present consequence. In a coda built around altered arrangements rather than restoration, that summary reads as timidity: the prose names the complication without fully inhabiting its emotional or physical texture.
